resolution_summary: The overlap is compatible. The PMC full-text candidate added publication-table adverse-event provenance and kept the D+Q bone safety synthesis boundary blocked; the headache candidate builds on that boundary by adding a term-specific structured headache result while preserving the same pooling_blocked safety constraints. The current synthesis_group record contains both updates without replacing or contradicting the broader PMC candidate.

evidence.summary: The broader PMC full-text candidate proposes the same safety synthesis group path to add publication adverse-event table provenance and safety pooling blockers.
The headache candidate proposes the same safety synthesis group path to add the headache term-specific result and preserve zero/not-reported blockers.
The current synthesis group includes the aggregate safety result, adverse-event term table result, headache term-specific result, full-text provenance, and pooling_blocked rationale.
